title: The Shooter Must Be Found
description: On pages 1–4, this part focuses on the shooter must be found, keeping the scene centered on the conflict, choices, and stakes of the Alabasta arc.
scene: The Shooter Must Be Found. The crew and Alabasta’s defenders race against time to stop the cannon before the city is destroyed. This section presents the immediate scene beat before the dialogue continues.

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: Find the one shooting the bomb!?

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: There's no time to think! The bomb's gonna go off in just 10 minutes, right?

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: But if this bomb has a blast radius of 5km, then the shooter might be aiming from a place at least 2.5km away!

dialogue.speaker: Chaka
dialogue.text: Or at least... I don't think so... The shooter is most likely... Very close to the palace square.

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: But why!? He'd only get caught up in the explo-

dialogue.speaker: Chaka
dialogue.text: ...That's just the kind of person Crocodile is.

dialogue.speaker: King Cobra
dialogue.text: He doesn't care if his agents die...!?

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: Then we should hurry up and-

dialogue.speaker: Miss All Sunday
dialogue.text: I wonder how high I'll be promoted once I kill you!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: We don't have time to waste by talking.

dialogue.speaker: Alabasta rebel
dialogue.text: Let's split up! But first, we need to get out from this damn dust cloud!

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: This is it... the very last time limit...

summary: This part develops the shooter must be found and pushes the Alabasta conflict closer to its next decisive turn.
